title: Remove A11y icon from header, darken all text site-wide for high-contrast readability

requirements.summary: Remove the Accessibility (A11y) icon/button from the header's icon row between Language switcher and AI Chatbot, then adjust spacing and alignment of remaining controls
acceptanceCriteria: The A11y icon is no longer visible in the header on any viewport size.
Remaining header controls (Size, EN, DSOUZA AI, Voice) are evenly spaced and visually balanced after removal.
No broken layout, gaps, or misalignment on desktop, tablet, or mobile.

file_operations.description: Remove the AccessibilityControls component import and JSX usage from the header's controls section. The A11y dropdown currently sits between LanguageSwitcher and the DSOUZA AI chatbot trigger button. After removal, adjust the gap/spacing in the flex container holding Size, EN, DSOUZA AI, and Voice controls to maintain visual balance. Ensure responsive behavior remains intact across desktop (flex-row), tablet, and mobile (likely flex-col or wrapped) viewports.

requirements.summary: Make all header text labels (Size, EN, DSOUZA AI, Voice) darker and highly readable using #000000 or #1A1A1A
acceptanceCriteria: All text labels in the header icon/control row render in #000000 or #1A1A1A.
Labels are clearly legible against the header background at all viewport sizes.
Contrast ratio meets WCAG AA standard (minimum 4.5:1 for normal text).

file_operations.description: Update the text color classes for all header control labels (Size font selector button text, language switcher button text showing 'EN', DSOUZA AI button label, and Voice assistant dropdown trigger text). Replace any existing text-muted-foreground, text-gray-600, or similar light color classes with text-black (which maps to #000000) or create a custom text-[#1A1A1A] class. Apply these dark text classes to desktop and mobile nav menu item text as well to ensure consistency.

Update the trigger button text color to use text-black or text-[#1A1A1A] instead of any existing muted or gray variant. Ensure the language code label (EN, HI, MR, etc.) is clearly legible with high contrast against the header background.
Update the Voice dropdown trigger button text color to use text-black or text-[#1A1A1A] for the 'Voice' label. Ensure it matches the other header controls for visual consistency and readability.

requirements.summary: Apply high-contrast dark text colors (#000000 for primary, #1A1A1A or #222222 for secondary) across the entire website, updating global CSS custom properties and Tailwind config
acceptanceCriteria: All page headings and subheadings render in #000000 or a near-black equivalent.
All body/paragraph text renders in #000000 or #1A1A1A.
Navigation menu items (desktop and mobile) render in high-contrast dark text.
Button labels are dark and readable (meeting WCAG AA contrast on their backgrounds).
Footer text (links, copyright, labels) is rendered in dark, high-contrast color.
No text element across Home, Services, About, Neo, or Contact pages uses a light-grey or low-contrast color that fails WCAG AA.
The soft light background of the site is preserved; only text foreground colors are darkened.
Layout and design remain elegant, minimal, and suitable for a professional banking platform.
All changes are responsive and look correct on desktop, tablet, and mobile.

file_operations.description: Update the CSS custom properties for foreground text colors in the :root selector. Change --foreground from its current OKLCH light value to oklch(0% 0 0) which represents pure black #000000. Update --muted-foreground to oklch(10% 0 0) for #1A1A1A equivalent. Also update --card-foreground, --popover-foreground, and any other text-related custom properties to use the same high-contrast dark values. Preserve all background color properties (--background, --card, --popover) to maintain the light premium theme. Ensure these changes apply in light mode; dark mode values can remain as-is since they already use light text on dark backgrounds.

Review and update the extended color palette if any custom text color utilities are defined (e.g., colors.primary.foreground, colors.secondary.foreground). Ensure these reference the updated CSS custom properties from index.css. If the config defines specific text colors that override the custom properties, update them to #000000 for primary and #1A1A1A or #222222 for secondary text to match the global dark text strategy.

Replace any text-muted-foreground, text-gray-500, text-gray-600, or similar low-contrast text color classes on footer links, headings, copyright text, and social media labels with text-black or text-[#1A1A1A]. Ensure all footer text meets WCAG AA contrast ratio against the footer background. Maintain the existing layout and spacing; only update text color classes.

Scan all text elements (hero heading, subheading, feature card titles and descriptions, testimonial text, stats labels, section headings) and replace any light or muted text color classes (text-muted-foreground, text-gray-600, text-gray-500) with text-black for headings and text-[#1A1A1A] for body text. Ensure button labels within CTAs use dark text with sufficient contrast on their background colors. Preserve all layout, animations, and background styling.

Update all headings, tab labels, card titles, feature list items, and descriptive text to use text-black or text-[#1A1A1A] instead of any muted or gray variants. Ensure product card text and category labels are highly readable with WCAG AA compliant contrast. Maintain responsive tab layout and card grid structure.

Replace any low-contrast text color classes in the story section, values cards, and milestones timeline with text-black for headings and text-[#1A1A1A] for body text. Ensure all narrative content is clearly legible. Preserve MotionReveal animations and responsive layout.

Update hero heading, feature descriptions, life-stage capability text, and CTA button labels to use high-contrast dark text colors (text-black or text-[#1A1A1A]). Ensure marketing copy is highly readable against the page background. Maintain visual hierarchy and responsive design.

Darken all text in the hero section, contact info cards (address, phone, email, hours), form labels, placeholder text (via CSS if possible), and submit button label using text-black or text-[#1A1A1A] classes. Ensure form inputs have sufficient contrast for accessibility. Preserve form validation, loading states, and toast notifications.

Update the brand promise heading, stats labels and values, and CTA button text to use high-contrast colors. Since this banner has a dark navy background, verify that the existing light text provides sufficient contrast; if the background is light, apply text-black. Adjust only if needed to meet WCAG AA standards. Preserve decorative blur elements and MotionReveal animation.
Ensure stat value numbers and labels render in text-black or text-[#1A1A1A] for maximum readability. Replace any muted or gray text classes. Maintain count-up animation and reduced-motion support.

Update trust card headings and descriptions to use text-black for titles and text-[#1A1A1A] for body text. Ensure all security and compliance content is clearly legible. Preserve hover animations and card layout.

Darken metric labels, values, and any descriptive text within the wellbeing tiles using text-black or text-[#1A1A1A]. Ensure color-coded icons remain visually distinct while text meets contrast standards. Maintain hover animations.

Update all labels (loan amount, interest rate, term), computed result labels (monthly EMI, total payment, total interest), and values to use high-contrast dark text. Replace any muted text classes with text-black or text-[#1A1A1A]. Preserve slider interactivity and responsive layout.

Darken card titles (Rural, Senior, Digital, Business) and any descriptive text within the experience selection cards using text-black. Ensure selected state styling maintains sufficient contrast. Preserve animated selection feedback and context updates.

Update savings goal tracker labels, achievement badge text, investment step guide headings, and credit score simulator labels to use text-black or text-[#1A1A1A]. Ensure gamified content is highly readable. Maintain interactive slider functionality.

Darken home loan tracker text, education cost calculator labels and values, tax-saving dashboard headings, and investment progress text using high-contrast colors (text-black or text-[#1A1A1A]). Ensure all financial data is clearly legible. Preserve slider interactivity and multi-widget layout.

Update pension/FD product card titles and descriptions, accessibility control labels, and fraud alert dialog text to use text-black or text-[#1A1A1A]. Ensure senior-focused content is highly readable. Maintain large font and high contrast toggle functionality.

Darken all feature card headings (low-data mode, voice banking, SMS banking, government schemes, micro-loans, crop insurance) and descriptions using text-black or text-[#1A1A1A]. Ensure rural banking content is clearly legible. Preserve adaptive layout for rural life stage context.

Update insurance product card titles, expandable content text, protection score quiz labels, and claim process step descriptions to use high-contrast dark text (text-black or text-[#1A1A1A]). Ensure all insurance and security information is highly readable. Maintain expandable card interactions.

Darken score labels (savings, investment, insurance, emergency fund), percentage values, and AI recommendation text using text-black or text-[#1A1A1A]. Ensure dashboard metrics are clearly legible. Preserve animated circular progress indicators.

Update chatbot message text (user and bot), input placeholder, and any button labels to use text-black or text-[#1A1A1A] for maximum readability. Ensure message bubbles have sufficient contrast with their backgrounds. Maintain typing indicator animation and Web Speech API integration.

Darken chat message text, language selector labels, font size control labels, and any system messages using text-black or text-[#1A1A1A]. Ensure multilingual conversation interface is highly readable. Preserve sliding panel animation and keyword-based bot responses.

Update button label, confirmation dialog heading, body text, and action button labels to use high-contrast dark text (text-black or text-[#1A1A1A]). Ensure emergency content is clearly legible. Maintain loading state and success toast notification.
Darken pill button labels (Youth, Family, Senior, Rural) using text-black or ensure selected/unselected states have sufficient contrast. Maintain context updates and visual selection feedback.
